nodes.label: Gary Nolan (extended)
nodes.summary: Wellington Nolan Research Lab at Stanford. Microbiologist & geneticist; spun up multiple nine-figure-exit companies. Has UFO parts in locked bank account. CIA tasked him with analyzing MRIs of UAP experiencers and Havana-syndrome cases — found distinctive caudate-putamen neural density pattern.

sources.quote: Stanford microbiologist; CIA-tasked UFO researcher; UFO parts custodian

nodes.label: Caudate-Putamen UAP-Experiencer Pattern
nodes.summary: Distinctive structural pattern Nolan identified between the caudate nucleus head and putamen — increased neural density, larger than normal — found across UAP experiencers, Havana-syndrome cases, and highly intelligent intuitive decision-makers. Genetically inherited (family members show pattern).

sources.timestamp_end: Distinctive brain structure in UAP experiencers and Havana-syndrome cases

nodes.label: Sensory Organs As Reductive Filter
nodes.summary: Nolan's hypothesis (shared with Jesse): the sensory organs are not productive but REDUCTIVE on a default state of near-omniscience — filter preventing brain overwhelm. UAP perception anomaly may be a widening of this filter.

sources.timestamp_end: Nolan's model: senses as reductive filter on greater omniscience

nodes.label: The Invisible College (Vallee / UFO science network)
nodes.summary: Jacques Vallee's term (from his 1975 book) for the informal network of credentialed UFO researchers working outside mainstream academia — including Vallee, Puthoff, Davis, Bigelow, Kolm Kelleher, Nolan.

sources.timestamp_end: Vallee's term for the UFO-research informal scientific network

nodes.label: Nolan states on camera he has UFO parts in a locked bank account — from cases like a fisherman witne
nodes.summary: Nolan states on camera he has UFO parts in a locked bank account — from cases like a fisherman witnessing a glowing object that exploded.

sources.quote: I mean, I have some in a locked bank account. A fisherman saw a glowing object and it just suddenly exploded.

type_specific.statement_text: Nolan states on camera he has UFO parts in a locked bank account — from cases like a fisherman witnessing a glowing object that exploded.

nodes.label: CIA tasked Nolan with analyzing MRIs of UAP experiencers alongside Havana-syndrome cases — he found
nodes.summary: CIA tasked Nolan with analyzing MRIs of UAP experiencers alongside Havana-syndrome cases — he found a common increased caudate-putamen neural density pattern across both populations.

sources.quote: The CIA came to my office. They said we had asked around and everybody said you've built the best tool called CITO. They showed me MRIs of some of these people. Most of those people had interactions with UFOs. We noticed an area of the brain that seemed to be disturbed — between the head of the caudate and the putamen, increased neural density.

type_specific.statement_text: CIA tasked Nolan with analyzing MRIs of UAP experiencers alongside Havana-syndrome cases — he found a common increased caudate-putamen neural density pattern across both populations.

nodes.label: The caudate-putamen pattern is heritable — Nolan found the same pattern in experiencers' family memb
nodes.summary: The caudate-putamen pattern is heritable — Nolan found the same pattern in experiencers' family members, indicating a genetic component to the predisposition for UAP perception.

sources.quote: Surprisingly, when we looked in the family members, we found that the family members had it. Which was fascinating. So that means that structure had a genetic component.

type_specific.statement_text: The caudate-putamen pattern is heritable — Nolan found the same pattern in experiencers' family members, indicating a genetic component to the predisposition for UAP perception.

nodes.label: Upon CIA contact, Nolan was 'introduced to others — what you people call the invisible college' — a
nodes.summary: Upon CIA contact, Nolan was 'introduced to others — what you people call the invisible college' — a network including Jacques Vallee, Hal Puthoff, Eric Davis, Robert Bigelow, and Colm Kelleher.

sources.quote: I was introduced to others who were I think you people call them the invisible college. It was people like Jacques, people like Hal Puthoff, Eric Davis and Robert Bigelow and Colm Kelleher.

type_specific.statement_text: Upon CIA contact, Nolan was 'introduced to others — what you people call the invisible college' — a network including Jacques Vallee, Hal Puthoff, Eric Davis, Robert Bigelow, and Colm Kelleher.

nodes.label: Nolan endorses the 'sensory organs as reductive filter' model — default state is near-omniscient, li
nodes.summary: Nolan endorses the 'sensory organs as reductive filter' model — default state is near-omniscient, limited perception protects us from cognitive overwhelm; UAP perception = filter widening.

sources.quote: Our senses are a filter to stop our brains from being overwhelmed with reality. The sensory organs are not necessarily productive. They're reductive on a default state of almost greater omniscience but an inability to make sense of right things.

type_specific.statement_text: Nolan endorses the 'sensory organs as reductive filter' model — default state is near-omniscient, limited perception protects us from cognitive overwhelm; UAP perception = filter widening.

nodes.label: Nolan notes AATIP's $22M budget contrasts absurdly with ~$100B fighter-jet budgets — discovering NHI
nodes.summary: Nolan notes AATIP's $22M budget contrasts absurdly with ~$100B fighter-jet budgets — discovering NHI and propulsion science gets < 1% of F-35 spending.

sources.quote: AATIP has a $22 million budget. Just compare that to fighter jet budgets which often exceed hundred billion. Discovering extraterrestrial life and propulsion gets less than 1% of the current F-35 budget.

type_specific.statement_text: Nolan notes AATIP's $22M budget contrasts absurdly with ~$100B fighter-jet budgets — discovering NHI and propulsion science gets < 1% of F-35 spending.

Episode: “These Are Parts Of an Alien UFO!” -Stanford Scientist Garry Nolan
Nolan states on camera he has UFO parts in a locked bank account — from cases like a fisherman witnessing a glowing object that exploded.
CIA tasked Nolan with analyzing MRIs of UAP experiencers alongside Havana-syndrome cases — he found a common increased caudate-putamen neural density pattern across both populations.
The caudate-putamen pattern is heritable — Nolan found the same pattern in experiencers' family members, indicating a genetic component to the predisposition for UAP perception.
Upon CIA contact, Nolan was 'introduced to others — what you people call the invisible college' — a network including Jacques Vallee, Hal Puthoff, Eric Davis, Robert Bigelow, and Colm Kelleher.
Nolan endorses the 'sensory organs as reductive filter' model — default state is near-omniscient, limited perception protects us from cognitive overwhelm; UAP perception = filter widening.
Nolan notes AATIP's $22M budget contrasts absurdly with ~$100B fighter-jet budgets — discovering NHI and propulsion science gets < 1% of F-35 spending.
